_meta: Regional variant data. Only languages with meaningful written standard variants are listed. If a language code is absent, set regionalCoherence.applicable = false.

fr-FR.note: Metropolitan French (fr-FR) expected. Flag Québécois markers (fr-CA: 'char', 'magasiner', 'blonde', interrogative 'tu' particle) or Swiss/Belgian archaisms if found.

es-ES.note: Peninsular Spanish expected. Flag Latin American markers: voseo (vos + conjugation), heavy LATAM vocabulary ('camión' for bus, 'celular' for phone), absence of 'vosotros'.

es-419.note: Latin American Spanish generic (es-419). Sub-variant UNCERTAIN unless recruiter specifies. Flag mixing of distinct sub-variants (e.g., Argentine voseo + Mexican vocabulary). Do NOT force assignment — flag as UNCERTAIN if region unclear.

pt-PT.note: European Portuguese expected. Flag Brazilian markers: gerund constructions ('estou fazendo' instead of 'estou a fazer'), informal 'você' instead of 'tu', Brazilian vocabulary ('ônibus' not 'autocarro', 'trem' not 'comboio').

pt-BR.note: Brazilian Portuguese expected. Flag European markers: perifrástica com 'a' + infinitivo ('estou a fazer'), 'tu' as informal pronoun with European conjugation, European vocabulary ('autocarro', 'comboio', 'frigorífico').

zh-CN.note: Simplified Chinese (Mainland) expected. Flag Traditional characters, Taiwan-specific vocabulary ('軟體' not '软件', '計程車' not '出租车'), or Cantonese-specific sentence-final particles.

zh-TW.note: Traditional Chinese (Taiwan, TOCFL standard) expected. Flag Simplified characters or Mainland-specific phrasing ('软件' not '軟體', '手机' not '手機', '出租车' not '計程車').

zh-HK.note: Traditional Chinese (Hong Kong context) expected. Flag: Simplified characters (error); Mainland-only phrasing without Cantonese awareness; absence of expected Cantonese particles (囉, 喎, 咋) in informal register. Note: formal HK written Chinese can be similar to zh-TW.

ar-AA.note: Modern Standard Arabic (فصحى) expected unless recruiter specifies a dialect. Flag MSA/dialect code-switching. Dialect UNCERTAIN unless briefed — do NOT force assignment. Note which dialectal markers are present (Egyptian: 'عايز/بتاع'; Levantine: 'هيك/منيح'; Gulf: 'وين/كيف'; Maghrebi: 'واش/بزاف').

no-NO.note: Bokmål (nb) is the default and most common written standard. Nynorsk (nn) is a fully distinct written standard — mixing forms from both in the same text is an error. If text appears to be Nynorsk throughout, note it as a regional written standard choice, not an error.

ru-RU.note: Standard Russian (ru-RU) expected. Flag Ukrainian-influenced forms (surzhyk patterns) or Belarussian-specific constructions if found.

de-DE.note: Standard German (de-DE / Hochdeutsch) expected. Austrian (de-AT: 'Jänner' for January, 'Matura' for 'Abitur') and Swiss (de-CH: no 'ß', 'ss' used instead; 'Grüezi', 'Velo') are legitimate variants — flag only if a specific variant was expected and another delivered.
